---
title: "Coordinates"
engine: julia
---

# Coordinates

## LonLat

`LonLat(lon, lat)` is GlobalGrids' coordinate type — a point on the Earth's surface in degrees (EPSG:4326).  It implements GeoInterface's `PointTrait`, so it works with plotting and spatial libraries.

```julia
using GlobalGrids

p = LonLat(-75.0, 54.0)

p.lon   # -75.0
p.lat   # 54.0
p[1]    # -75.0 (indexable)
p[2]    # 54.0
```

A `LonLat` can also be constructed from a tuple:

```julia
LonLat((1.0, 2.0))
```

## Distance and Bearings

- `haversine(a, b)` — great-circle distance in meters using the Haversine formula.
- `azimuth(a, b)` — initial bearing from `a` to `b`, degrees clockwise from North.
- `destination(origin, azimuth°, dist_m)` — destination point at the given azimuth and distance.

```julia
using GlobalGrids

a = LonLat(-75.0, 54.0)
b = LonLat(-80.0, 50.0)

GlobalGrids.haversine(a, b)              # distance in meters
GlobalGrids.azimuth(a, b)                # bearing in degrees
GlobalGrids.destination(a, 0.0, 111_195) # ~1° north
```

These functions also work directly on cell types:

```julia
c1 = H3Cell(a, 5)
c2 = H3Cell(b, 5)
GlobalGrids.haversine(c1, c2)  # distance between centroids
```
